history.qwen_observation: Compilation failed after Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade because Hibernate 5 internal API CurrentTimestampFunction was removed/relocated in Hibernate 6.
history.qwen_rationale: Spring Boot 3.x pulls in Hibernate 6.x, which restructured internal packages and removed legacy function classes. Inserting MigrateToHibernate62 immediately after the Spring Boot upgrade will rewrite the Hibernate-specific source code to use Hibernate 6-compatible APIs, resolving the compilation error before proceeding to Java 17/21 transforms.

history.qwen_observation: The hibernate_6_migration step prematurely converted javax.persistence imports to jakarta.persistence, causing compilation failures because the project still depended on Spring Boot 2.6.6 (which provides javax.persistence).
history.qwen_rationale: Removing the standalone hibernate_6_migration step prevents the premature namespace switch. The UpgradeSpringBoot_3_3 meta-recipe will handle the javax-to-jakarta migration, Hibernate 5-to-6 upgrade, and Spring Security adapter removal atomically under JDK 17, ensuring dependencies and source code stay in sync during the transition.

history.qwen_observation: Compilation fails on JDK 17 after Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade because Task.java imports and uses org.hibernate.query.criteria.internal.expression.function.CurrentTimestampFunction, an internal Hibernate 5 class removed in Hibernate 6.
history.qwen_rationale: Switching to MigrateToHibernate62 provides broader coverage for Hibernate 5-to-6 API changes, including internal class removals like CurrentTimestampFunction. Running it atomically with UpgradeSpringBoot_3_3 on JDK 17 ensures the source is rewritten before the compiler encounters the missing package, while preserving the required Java 17 baseline for Spring Boot 3.x.
